title: When Mountains Leapt

sections.label: Intro
lines: The waters rose in solemn flight,
Mountains danced in ancient light.
The earth itself began to breathe,
A trembling song beneath our feet.

sections.label: Verse 1
lines: When Israel walked, unshackled, free,
A foreign land gave way to sea.
The Jordan split, a path was made,
A fearful tide at His parade.

sections.label: Chorus
lines: Why did you flee, O sea of despair?
Why did you leap, O mountains so bare?
The trembling earth, in awe, it cried,
Before the God who turns stone to life.

sections.label: Verse 2
lines: He carved the streams from rock-bound chains,
He loosed the waters from dry plains.
In His shadow, nations quake,
Their idols fall, their strongholds break.

sections.label: Bridge
lines: O mountains high, O valleys low,
You bow to Him, the one you know.
His voice is thunder, soft yet clear,
The earth responds, it draws Him near.

sections.label: Chorus
lines: Why did you flee, O sea of despair?
Why did you leap, O mountains so bare?
The trembling earth, in awe, it cried,
Before the God who turns stone to life.

sections.label: Outro
lines: The waters whisper, the mountains sigh,
His presence lingers, His name will rise.
Forever marked in earth and flame,
The trembling world will know His name.

styleNote: Layered With Melancholic Strings · Swelling With Emotion · Full-Band Crescendo
